org.arakhne.neteditor.fig.figure

Class AbstractFigure

  • java.lang.Object
    • org.arakhne.neteditor.formalism.AbstractPropertyTooler
      • org.arakhne.neteditor.fig.view.AbstractViewComponent
        • org.arakhne.neteditor.fig.figure.AbstractFigure
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    Serializable, Comparable<ViewComponent>, Selectable, PropertyOwner, Figure, ViewComponent, ViewComponentConstants, PropertyNames
    Direct Known Subclasses:
    AbstractModelObjectFigure, DecorationFigure

    public abstract class AbstractFigure
extends AbstractViewComponent
implements Figure
    This class is the base class for basic drawing objects such as rectangles, lines, text, circles, etc.
    Version:
    17.0 2013-10-23 21:03:01
    Author:
    Stéphane GALLAND, Marc BAUMGARTNER
    See Also:
    Serialized Form
    Maven Group Id:
    org.arakhne.neteditor
    Maven Artifact Id:
    neteditor-fig

    • Constructor Detail

      • AbstractFigure

        public AbstractFigure(UUID viewUUID,
              float x,
              float y,
              float width,
              float height)
        Construct a new Fig.

        The specified width and height are set inconditionally. The minimal width becomes the min between the specified width and the ViewComponentConstants.DEFAULT_MINIMAL_SIZE. The minimal height becomes the min between the specified height and the ViewComponentConstants.DEFAULT_MINIMAL_SIZE. The maximal width becomes the max between the specified width and the ViewComponentConstants.DEFAULT_MAXIMAL_SIZE. The maximal height becomes the max between the specified height and the ViewComponentConstants.DEFAULT_MAXIMAL_SIZE.

        Parameters:
        viewUUID - is the UUID of the view that is enclosing this figure.
        x - horizontal position of this Fig.
        y - vertical position of this Fig.
        width - width of this Fig.
        height - height of this Fig.

    • Method Detail

      • avoidCollision

        protected void avoidCollision()
        Move this figure to avoid collision with the other figures.

        public Set<ResizeDirection> getResizeDirections()

      • setResizeDirections

        public void setResizeDirections(ResizeDirection... resizeDirections)
        Set the directions the figure can be resized by the user
        Specified by:
        setResizeDirections in interface Figure
        Parameters:
        resizeDirections - the new directions. If none was given, the shape cannot be resized.

        Set the directions the figure can be resized by the user to all the available directions.

        Returns:

        Parameters:

      • setLocked

        public void setLocked(boolean lock)
        Set the lock-state of this Fig. When a Fig is locked, it should not be moved by the user.
        Specified by:
        setLocked in interface Figure
        Parameters:
        lock - true if this Fig is locked.

      • isLocked

        public boolean isLocked()
        Reply the lock-state of this Fig. When a Fig is locked, it should not be moved by the user.
        Specified by:
        isLocked in interface Figure
        Returns:
        true if this Fig is locked.

      • isAssociatedFiguresAutoLocked

        public boolean isAssociatedFiguresAutoLocked()
        Reply the lock-state of the associated figures is automatically updated when the lock-state of this figure changed.
        Specified by:
        isAssociatedFiguresAutoLocked in interface Figure
        Returns:
        true if this lock-state of the associated figures changed as the lock-state of this figure change; false otherwise.

      • setAssociatedFiguresAutoLocked

        public void setAssociatedFiguresAutoLocked(boolean autoLock)
        Set if the lock-state of the associated figures is automatically updated when the lock-state of this figure changed.
        Specified by:
        setAssociatedFiguresAutoLocked in interface Figure
        Parameters:
        autoLock - is true if this lock-state of the associated figures changed as the lock-state of this figure change; false otherwise.

      • getShadowPainter

        public ShadowPainter getShadowPainter()
        Replies the painter that may be used to paint this figure in shadow mode. You must call ShadowPainter.release() when you have finished to paint the figure in shadow mode.
        Specified by:
        getShadowPainter in interface Figure
        Returns:
        the painter, never null

      • createShadowPainter

        protected abstract ShadowPainter createShadowPainter()
        Invoked by getShadowPainter() when an instance of shadow painter must be create.
        Returns:
        the instance of shadow painter.
